title: azurinia aphylla
intro: Azurinia aphylla, also known as the desert tea, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and silvery-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. Azurinia aphylla is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
